name: estregenesis
description: The EG kit plugin — the entry point to EstreGenesis itself, as skills instead of a copy-pasted URL. 7 skills over 4 canonical procedures: eg-bootstrap (/egboot — new project: pick the seed tier, install it as .agent/seed_prompt.md, run the seed's Bootstrap mode) · eg-migration (/egmig — existing project with its own rule files: audit them, classify M1/M3, run the seed's Migration A or C, promote every rule or record its drop) · eg-upgrade (/egup — seeded project: read the seed's version marker, diff it against the upstream CHANGELOG, present an additive numbered delta menu, then refresh the installed EG plugins) · egrich (full arm: the seed plus all six optional modules, their plugins, hooks and MCP servers, wired and verified — with the cheap discipline-only subset named up front so 'everything' is a choice, not a default). The three short names are pointer aliases, not copies (Compendium's pointer-not-paraphrase discipline applied to the plugin's own surface): each alias reads the canonical sibling skill and executes it. Seed locations (raw.githubusercontent.com, 3 tiers x 2 languages) are baked into the skills, which is the whole point — the seed is fetched, never reconstructed from memory, and its version marker is preserved so a later upgrade can diff mechanically. 0 npm deps, no hooks, no MCP server: this plugin is procedure, not runtime.
